matches.text: theorem det_diagonal {d : n → R} : det (diagonal d) = ∏ i, d i := by
matches.snippet:
theorem det_diagonal {d : n → R} : det (diagonal d) = ∏ i, d i := by
  rw [det_apply']
  refine (Finset.sum_eq_single 1 ?_ ?_).trans ?_
  · rintro σ - h2
    obtain ⟨x, h3⟩ := not_forall.1 (mt Equiv.ext h2)
    convert mul_zero (ε σ)
    apply Finset.prod_eq_zero (mem_univ x)
    exact if_neg h3
  · simp
  · simp

matches.text: theorem diagonal_apply {α ι} [Nonempty ι] (x : α) : diagonal α ι x = fun _ => x := by
matches.snippet:
theorem diagonal_apply {α ι} [Nonempty ι] (x : α) : diagonal α ι x = fun _ => x := by
  ext; simp [diagonal]

/-- The **Hales-Jewett theorem**. This version has a restriction on universe levels which is
necessary for the proof. See `exists_mono_in_high_dimension` for a fully universe-polymorphic
version. -/

matches.text: theorem diagonal_apply [Zero α] (d : n → α) (i j) : diagonal d i j = if i = j then d i else 0 :=
matches.snippet:
theorem diagonal_apply [Zero α] (d : n → α) (i j) : diagonal d i j = if i = j then d i else 0 :=
  rfl

@[simp]

matches.text: theorem prod_congr (f g : α → M) (h : ∀ a, f a = g a) : ∏ a, f a = ∏ a, g a :=
matches.snippet:
theorem prod_congr (f g : α → M) (h : ∀ a, f a = g a) : ∏ a, f a = ∏ a, g a :=
  Finset.prod_congr rfl fun a _ha => h a

@[to_additive]

matches.text: theorem prod_congr' {a b : ℕ} (f : Fin b → M) (h : a = b) :
matches.snippet:
theorem prod_congr' {a b : ℕ} (f : Fin b → M) (h : a = b) :
    (∏ i : Fin a, f (i.cast h)) = ∏ i : Fin b, f i := by
  subst h
  congr

@[to_additive]

matches.text: theorem prod_congr {f : α →₀ M} {g1 g2 : α → M → N} (h : ∀ x ∈ f.support, g1 x (f x) = g2 x (f x)) :
matches.snippet:
theorem prod_congr {f : α →₀ M} {g1 g2 : α → M → N} (h : ∀ x ∈ f.support, g1 x (f x) = g2 x (f x)) :
    f.prod g1 = f.prod g2 :=
  Finset.prod_congr rfl h

@[to_additive]

matches.text: theorem sign_swap {x y : α} (h : x ≠ y) : sign (swap x y) = -1 :=
matches.snippet:
theorem sign_swap {x y : α} (h : x ≠ y) : sign (swap x y) = -1 :=
  (signAux3_mul_and_swap 1 1 _ mem_univ).2 h

@[simp]

matches.text: theorem sign_swap' {x y : α} : sign (swap x y) = if x = y then 1 else -1 :=
matches.snippet:
theorem sign_swap' {x y : α} : sign (swap x y) = if x = y then 1 else -1 :=
  if H : x = y then by simp [H, swap_self] else by simp [sign_swap H, H]

matches.text: theorem sign_mul (x y : EReal) : sign (x * y) = sign x * sign y := by
matches.snippet:
theorem sign_mul (x y : EReal) : sign (x * y) = sign x * sign y := by
  induction x, y using induction₂_symm_neg with
  | top_zero => simp only [mul_zero, sign_zero]
  | top_top => rfl
  | symm h => rwa [mul_comm, EReal.mul_comm]
  | coe_coe => simp only [← coe_mul, sign_coe, _root_.sign_mul]
  | top_pos _ h =>
    rw [top_mul_coe_of_pos h, sign_top, one_mul, sign_pos (EReal.coe_pos.2 h)]
  | neg_left h => rw [neg_mul, sign_neg, sign_neg, h, neg_mul]

@[simp] protected theorem sign_mul_abs : ∀ x : EReal, (sign x * x.abs : EReal) = x
  | ⊥ => by simp
  | ⊤ => by simp
  | (x : ℝ) => by rw [sign_coe, coe_abs, ← coe_coe_sign, ← coe_mul, sign_mul_abs]

@[simp] protected theorem abs_mul_sign (x : EReal) : (x.abs * sign x : EReal) = x := by
  rw [EReal.mul_comm, EReal.sign_mul_abs]

matches.text: theorem sign_mul (x y : α) : sign (x * y) = sign x * sign y := by
matches.snippet:
theorem sign_mul (x y : α) : sign (x * y) = sign x * sign y := by
  rcases lt_trichotomy x 0 with (hx | hx | hx) <;> rcases lt_trichotomy y 0 with (hy | hy | hy) <;>
    simp [hx, hy, mul_pos_of_neg_of_neg, mul_neg_of_neg_of_pos, mul_neg_of_pos_of_neg]

@[simp] theorem sign_mul_abs (x : α) : (sign x * |x| : α) = x := by
  rcases lt_trichotomy x 0 with hx | rfl | hx <;> simp [*, abs_of_pos, abs_of_neg]

@[simp] theorem abs_mul_sign (x : α) : (|x| * sign x : α) = x := by
  rcases lt_trichotomy x 0 with hx | rfl | hx <;> simp [*, abs_of_pos, abs_of_neg]

@[simp]

matches.text: theorem sign_mul (f g : Perm α) : sign (f * g) = sign f * sign g :=
matches.snippet:
theorem sign_mul (f g : Perm α) : sign (f * g) = sign f * sign g :=
  map_mul sign f g

@[simp]

matches.text: Finset.sum_involution (fun σ _ => Equiv.swap i j • σ)
matches.snippet:
Finset.sum_involution (fun σ _ => Equiv.swap i j • σ)
          (fun σ _ => domCoprod.summand_add_swap_smul_eq_zero a b σ hv hij)
          (fun σ _ => mt <| domCoprod.summand_eq_zero_of_smul_invariant a b σ hv hij)
          (fun σ _ => Finset.mem_univ _) fun σ _ =>
          Equiv.swap_smul_involutive i j σ }

matches.text: Finset.sum_involution (fun σ _ => swap i j * σ)
matches.snippet:
Finset.sum_involution (fun σ _ => swap i j * σ)
      (fun σ _ => by simp [Perm.sign_swap i_ne_j, apply_swap_eq_self hv])
      (fun σ _ _ => (not_congr swap_mul_eq_iff).mpr i_ne_j) (fun σ _ => Finset.mem_univ _)
      fun σ _ => swap_mul_involutive i j σ

/-- Produce an `AlternatingMap` out of a `MultilinearMap`, by summing over all argument
permutations. -/

matches.text: from an identity implemented in mathlib as `Finset.sum_involution`. Namely, we use
matches.snippet:
from an identity implemented in mathlib as `Finset.sum_involution`. Namely, we use
`Finset.sum_involution` to show that `∑ t ∈ pairs σ k, weight σ R k t = 0`. We then identify
`(-1) ^ k * k * esymm σ R k` with the terms of the weight sum for which `t.fst` has
cardinality `k`, and `(-1) ^ i * esymm σ R i * psum σ R (k - i)` with the terms of the weight sum
for which `t.fst` has cardinality `i` for `i < k`, and we thereby derive the main result
`(-1) ^ k * k * esymm σ R k + ∑ i ∈ range k, (-1) ^ i * esymm σ R i * psum σ R (k - i) = 0` (or
rather, two equivalent forms which provide direct definitions for `esymm` and `psum` in lower-degree
terms).

## Main declarations

* `MvPolynomial.mul_esymm_eq_sum`: a recurrence relation for the `k`th elementary
  symmetric polynomial in terms of lower-degree elementary symmetric polynomials and power sums.

* `MvPolynomial.psum_eq_mul_esymm_sub_sum`: a recurrence relation for the degree-`k` power sum
  in terms of lower-degree elementary symmetric polynomials and power sums.

## References

See [zeilberger1984] for the combinatorial proof of Newton's identities.
-/

public section

open Equiv (Perm)

open MvPolynomial

matches.text: theorem mul_swap_involutive (i j : α) : Function.Involutive (· * Equiv.swap i j) :=
matches.snippet:
theorem mul_swap_involutive (i j : α) : Function.Involutive (· * Equiv.swap i j) :=
  mul_swap_mul_self i j

@[simp]
